product_overview: Trancetta Pizza House is a French-language restaurant website that showcases the brand, highlights signature pizzas, and lets visitors build a customized rectangular pizza tray in a client-side menu experience. The site also provides location and contact information for customers who want to learn more or visit in person.
core_goals: Present the restaurant brand and offering on the home page.
Let users explore the menu and build a customized pizza tray.

features.name: Home page discovery
features.description: Users can browse the restaurant’s landing page content and move into the menu experience from the home page.
user_flows: Open the home page -> Scroll through the promotional sections -> View the brand story and featured offerings -> View location and contact information -> Navigate to the menu page

features.name: Pizza menu builder
features.description: Users can choose a tray size, place pizzas into available zones, and review the current configuration in a responsive client-side builder.
user_flows: Open the menu page -> Select a tray size -> Drag a pizza from the sidebar onto an available tray zone -> Review the order summary
Open the menu page -> Select a tray size -> Use the mobile pizza selector to choose a pizza on a smaller screen -> Review the order summary

routes.description: Home page with hero, story, best sellers, gallery, location, and contact sections
Interactive pizza menu builder with drag-and-drop plate composition

features.name: Home page discovery
features.description: Browse the landing page sections and navigate into the menu experience.
user_interactions: Click the menu navigation link to open the menu page

features.name: Pizza menu builder
features.description: Configure a pizza plate by choosing a size and placing pizzas into the available zones.
user_interactions: Drag a pizza from the sidebar onto a plate zone
Use the mobile selector to choose a pizza on smaller screens
Review the order summary after making selections

known_limitations.issue: The menu builder is client-side only and there is no backend checkout or order persistence.
known_limitations.impact: Users can compose a plate in the UI, but the selection is not sent to a server.
